lines: + * Functional coverage for the wiredTigerRepair command. The read-only sub-commands dispatch to
+ * WiredTiger's wiredtiger_repair() interface; fixDatabaseSize runs a checkpoint with a debug config.
+ * This runs on a plain (non-disaggregated) standalone, so it covers what is exercisable there:
+ * fetchMetadata (which reads the local metadata cursor), sub-command validation, config-injection
+ * rejection, and that fixDatabaseSize is rejected off a disaggregated leader. The disaggregated
+ * paths (fetchDatabaseSize, fetchMetadata local=false, a successful fixDatabaseSize) are covered
+ * Lives in the disk_wiredtiger suite because the command requires the on-disk WiredTiger engine.
+    it("fetchMetadata returns real WiredTiger metadata", function () {

+        // The history store file exists on every WiredTiger instance.

+    it("treats fixDatabaseSize:false as not requesting the fix", function () {

// All commands applicable on timeseries collections in the server.
// A list of additional CRUD ops which exercise different write paths, and do error handling
whatsmysni: {skip: "does not accept read or write concern"},
+    wiredTigerRepair: {skip: "does not accept read or write concern"},
containerInsert: "containerInsert" # run "ci" ops in the applyOps command
containerDelete: "containerDelete" # run "cd" ops in the applyOps command

virtual Status autoCompact(RecoveryUnit&, const AutoCompactOptions& options) = 0;

* Pauses (pause=true) or resumes (pause=false) background auto-compaction for a replica set
* write block critical section transition. Pausing saves the currently-active configuration so
// Background auto-compaction reconfigures are applied asynchronously by the storage engine;
// while a previous reconfigure is still being consumed, a new one is transiently rejected with
Status autoCompact(RecoveryUnit&, const AutoCompactOptions& options) override;
# This is the smallest possible set of files that wraps WT
+    // wiredtiger_repair() has no error-code contract -- failures are reported as text in the
+    // (connection-owned, must-copy) report string, not via the return value.
+    // Valid only on a disaggregated leader (ENOTSUP otherwise).

+# The wiredTigerRepair command exposes WiredTiger's runtime maintenance operations. Each invocation
+# carries exactly one sub-command. Two mirror wiredtiger_repair() config keys (fetchDatabaseSize ->
+# fetch_database_size, fetchMetadata -> fetch_metadata); fixDatabaseSize instead triggers a
+# checkpoint with debug=(database_size_fix=true) rather than a wiredtiger_repair() call.
+        description: "Options for the fetch_database_size WiredTiger repair sub-command."
+                    Read the disaggregated database size (disagg-only). local=true (default) returns
+                    the in-memory maintained running total; local=false recomputes it by walking the
+        description: "Options for the fetch_metadata WiredTiger repair sub-command."
+                description: "Read the local metadata cursor rather than the shared checkpoint."
+                description: "Restrict the metadata fetch to this URI; absent means all URIs."
+                description: "Restrict the metadata fetch to this key; absent means the whole value."
+                    Diagnostic report from the sub-command (wiredtiger_repair() output for the fetch
+                    sub-commands, or a status string for fixDatabaseSize).
+            Invoke WiredTiger's runtime maintenance interface. Requires the wiredtiger action
+            (ActionType::wiredtiger), which is not held by any built-in role by default. Exactly one
+                description: "Return the disaggregated database size. Mirrors fetch_database_size."
+                    When true, recompute and persist the disaggregated database size via a
+                    checkpoint with debug=(database_size_fix=true) (not a wiredtiger_repair call).

+ * Assembles a WiredTiger configuration string, mirroring the WTConfig helper used by the native
+ * repair tooling: keys are comma-separated and nested groups are wrapped in parentheses. This keeps
+ * the command from splicing user input into a raw config string by hand.
+    WtConfigBuilder& append(std::string_view key, const WtConfigBuilder& nested) {
+ * Quotes a string value for inclusion in a WiredTiger config. The value becomes part of a config
+ * string parsed by WiredTiger, so validate it against an allow-list of the characters that appear
+ * in WiredTiger URIs and config keys (alphanumerics plus '.', '_', '-', ':' and '/') and reject
+ * everything else. An allow-list -- rather than blocking known-bad characters -- keeps the typed
+ * command surface from becoming a config-injection vector.
+                "wiredTigerRepair config value contains a disallowed character",
+// Builds the wiredtiger_repair() config for whichever read-only sub-command is present. The caller
+// (typedRun) has already enforced that exactly one of the fetch sub-commands is set.
+ * Command surface for WiredTiger's runtime maintenance operations. The typed fields are translated
+ * into the WiredTiger config string here rather than accepting a raw config, and the work is
+ * plumbed to the storage engine (never to WiredTigerKVEngine directly). Access is gated on
+ * ActionType::wiredtiger, which is not held by any built-in role by default.
+        return "WiredTiger maintenance command. Requires the 'wiredtiger' action privilege "
+               "(ActionType::wiredtiger), which is not held by any built-in role by default. "
+               "Provide exactly one of fetchDatabaseSize / fetchMetadata (read-only, via "
+               "wiredtiger_repair) or fixDatabaseSize (recomputes the disaggregated database size "
+    // Declare the authorization requirement (cluster resource + ActionType::wiredtiger) in the
+    // command's contract so it is discoverable from the command definition, not only enforced in
+    // doCheckAuthorization below. The IDL access_check generates this contract.
